title: Shopping
description: Malls, supermarkets, Indian grocery, electronics and Sunday markets in Belgium.

items.name: City2 Shopping Centre
items.description: Large shopping centre on Brussels' main pedestrian shopping street, Rue Neuve.

items.name: Wijnegem Shopping Center
items.description: One of Belgium's largest shopping malls, just outside Antwerp.

items.name: Midi Market / Marché du Midi
items.description: One of Europe's largest Sunday markets, with South Asian, North African and Mediterranean produce, spices and goods.

items.name: Indian grocery & electronics shops
items.description: A cluster of Indian grocery, spice and household goods shops serving the Brussels Indian community.

items.name: Antwerp Diamond District shops
items.description: Indian and Gujarati grocery shops serving the diamond-trading community, alongside the diamond trade itself.

items.name: Rue Neuve (Nieuwstraat)
items.description: Belgium's busiest shopping street — a pedestrian strip of mainstream fashion chains just steps from the Grand Place.

items.name: Avenue Louise
items.description: Brussels' premier luxury boulevard, lined with flagship stores for the world's top fashion houses.

items.name: Boulevard de Waterloo
items.description: A compact stretch of high-end boutiques neighbouring Avenue Louise, home to Belgium's own Delvaux leather house.

items.name: Rue Antoine Dansaert
items.description: The go-to street for independent Belgian fashion designers, concept stores and laid-back cafés.

items.name: Chaussée d'Ixelles
items.description: A busy, everyday shopping strip mixing fast fashion with independent lifestyle boutiques near Porte de Namur.

items.name: Meir
items.description: Belgium's busiest shopping street, running from Antwerp Central Station through a mix of mainstream retailers.

items.name: Schuttershofstraat
items.description: Antwerp's luxury shopping lane just off the Meir, home to top international fashion and watch houses.

items.name: Nationalestraat
items.description: The heart of Antwerp's fashion scene, anchored by Dries Van Noten and the Fashion Museum (MoMu).

items.name: Kammenstraat
items.description: Antwerp's alternative shopping street, known for streetwear, vintage and independent fashion stores.

items.name: Huidevettersstraat
items.description: A quieter premium fashion lane running parallel to the Meir, popular for its boutique shopping.

items.name: Veldstraat
items.description: Ghent's main shopping street, a pedestrian strip of mainstream fashion chains leading to Korenmarkt.

items.name: Brabantdam
items.description: A lifestyle and fashion street near Saint Bavo's Cathedral, dotted with independent local boutiques.

items.name: Langemunt
items.description: A retail street close to Gravensteen Castle, mixing fashion chains with local stores.

items.name: Steenstraat
items.description: Bruges' main shopping street linking the train station to Market Square, packed with chocolatiers, lace shops and fashion.

items.name: Geldmuntstraat
items.description: A charming boutique shopping street near the Belfry, good for fashion and local gifts.

items.name: Kapellestraat
items.description: Ostend's main seafront shopping street, mixing fashion, cafés and souvenir stores steps from the beach.

items.name: Bruul
items.description: Mechelen's main high street, close to Grote Markt, with a mix of fashion chains and local boutiques.

items.name: IJzerenleen
items.description: A boutique shopping street near St. Rumbold's Cathedral, home to specialty stores and cafés.
